methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Atom Computing is a Series B chip and semiconductor company that has raised $60M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private peers and 3 public semiconductors companies, including Etched ($5.0B, Series B), d-Matrix ($2.0B, Series C), and Positron AI ($1.0B, Series B) on the private side, and ALAB, NVDA, and AMD as public anchors.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $60M total raised, yielding $114M. Peer-Median valuation across the 6 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median of $1.0B, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied because Atom Computing's revenue is not on file, precluding a price-to-sales calculation against the public semiconductor median of 20.3x.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a valuation range of $97M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $557M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty: the stage-progression method anchors to realized capital, while peer-median reflects market multiples for later-stage frontier semiconductor companies. Revenue disclosure and a clearer revenue-to-capex ratio would narrow this range materially.
